steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The clothing-exclusive lice variant requires “ample ecological niches”—populations of humans wearing clothing. If correctly dated to 170Ka, it proves humans had established regular clothing use by then.

steps.title: Match the timing to when thermal necessity began
steps.content: The passage shows clothing was unnecessary in Africa but became essential outside Africa by 70Ka. The lice evidence places clothing at 170Ka—well before humans even left Africa. This means humans wore clothing long before they needed it for survival, exactly as choice C states. The other choices reverse the timeline, address non-modern hominids, or focus on post-70Ka factors. That matches choice C — C is correct.
